description: Using a mutable default argument (list/dict/set) so the single shared default object accumulates state across calls.
body: Failure mode: a function signature like def add(item, bucket=[]) creates one list at definition time that every call without an explicit bucket shares, so values leak between unrelated calls. Detection signals: a function returns data from a previous invocation; results depend on call history; a linter (ruff B006, pylint W0102) flags 'dangerous default value'. Do not use mutable literals as default arguments. Safer fallback: default to None and create the container inside the body (bucket = [] if bucket is None else bucket), then add a test that calls the function twice and asserts independence.
